Headline
A reliable and efficient Kindle charger with a few drawbacks.

Product summary
Users praise the charger's ability to quickly and effectively charge their Kindle devices. However, some find it disappointing that it does not charge faster than standard chargers and question the need to purchase it separately due to its high price.

Key features
- Fast charging capabilities: Delivers a quick charge to Kindle devices.
- Multi-device compatibility: Can be used to charge various Kindle models and other devices.
- Compact design: Offers a space-saving solution for charging needs.

Pros
- Effectively charges Kindle devices
- Suitable for charging multiple devices
- Compact and portable design

Cons
- Does not charge faster than standard chargers
- Considered expensive by some users
- Lacks inclusion in Kindle device packages

User experience
I was initially disappointed when I realized that the charger did not charge my Kindle faster than my previous one. However, I was pleased to find that it still delivered a reliable and efficient charge, and I appreciated its multi-device compatibility. The compact design was also a bonus, as it took up less space in my travel bag. Overall, while it may not be the fastest charger on the market, it is a reliable and effective option for charging my Kindle.

However, I do believe that the price is a drawback. It seems unfair to have to purchase a charger separately, especially when it does not offer any significant improvement over the charger that comes with the Kindle. I would have preferred if it were included in the device package or offered at a more reasonable price.

Despite these concerns, I have been satisfied with the performance of the charger and would recommend it to others in need of a reliable and efficient charging solution for their Kindle devices.

Stay away from the certified refurbished Amazon Fire TV. I received two Certified Refurbished defective fire TVs. The first the screen looked half corrupted, see the pictures. The second Certified Refurbished Amazon fire tv I received would not boot up, it was stuck in an infinite loop where I would only see the amazon logo for 10 seconds then it would boot up again.If anything, Amazon needs to re-visit its refurbishment process and quality control of the refurbished products they let get in consumer hands.Update 11/28/16: I purchased a new Fire TV during the BF sale and I have had no issues so far :). Both devices were all right but neither could handle 5.1 audio from Netflix on my Vizio sound bar (essentially your device must be cable of handling Dolby Digital Plus and nothing less using Roku). I finally decided to give FireTV a try and I'm amazed. Not only does it convert DD+ to DD on Netflix and HBO Go, I can install XBMC (Kodi) on it! I wish I knew about this before I bought my Roku 4 and HTPC. I now literally have no use for my HTPC anymore... FireTV can handle EVERYTHING now and all with DD and DTS 5.1 audio. Even better, I have a ton of smart devices and 3 Echos... I can now control all of these devices directly from the remote. This is seriously one of the best purchases I have made in the past year. Love it, had the stick and it was kinda slow navigating. This one is much faster. Was going to use this and cancel cable. When I told my cable company I wanted to cancel and I was just going to stream everything they cut my bundle cost by 100. I kept cable but I also still use my firetv alot. This box is wonderful, meets my expectations plus some. I gave up cable and tried it. I don't watch a lot of movies but this box comes with a lot of selections in my opinion more than basic cable, and if you have prime even more selections, theres a lot of free shows just download the apps for them. I bought this thinking since we'd bought several other refurbished things that worked, that it would be okay. The box never booted properly so the only thing we could stare at was the Amazon logo and the words Unknown Boot in the corner. Customer Service tried to help with the box but we couldn't get it to trouble shoot. Sending this box back. Certified garbage! Lesson learned about paying attention to reviews. Fire TV box and remote was packaged like new, even included new batteries. After plugging it in it would only show Amazon logo and 'unknown boot' on screen. Cycled power several times and even tried to reset using remote buttons, no dice. I read all the negative reviews before I purchased this, I chose to take the chance so I'm angry with myself, but very disappointed with Amazon for even suggesting this is refurbished, let alone certified, as if that means something. I bought this after being an avid Apple fan for the last 10 years. I had old style two Apple TV's and even bought a new Apple TV to help with me cutting the cord to DirectTV after 11 years. I switch to using the Apple TV with the DirecTV Now streaming service. After the Apple TV would no longer let friends or family airplay to it because of new updates or let me load any third party apps I knew it was time to switch. It was a great switch to the Fire TV and I have not looked back. I have been able to load numerous apps that are not available on the Apple TV such as Showbox and Kodi. My biggest complaint was the Fire TV remote was not able to control the volume on my sound bar like the Apple TV remote and was not able to turn my TV or sound bar on or off. The Sideclick remote was exactly what I needed. I was able to program the remote to control power and sound level, along with change the input on the TV. I would highly recommend using the Fire TV.Pros:Works extremely wellAllows 3rd party app installationMuch cheaper than Apple TVAllows use of a

Headline
A mixed experience with the Fire TV: Secure streaming and affordable price, but potential issues with refurbished models.

Product summary
The Fire TV offers secure streaming capabilities and an affordable price, but some users have reported issues with refurbished models.

Key features
- Secure streaming: Allows users to access a wide range of content, including Netflix and HBO Go, with DD+ to DD conversion.
- Affordable price: Offers a cost-effective alternative to traditional cable or Apple TV.
- 3rd party app installation: Enables users to download additional apps, such as Kodi and Showbox.

Pros
- Secure and reliable streaming capabilities
- Wide range of available content
- Affordable price point

Cons
- Potential issues with refurbished models, including defects and lack of functionality

User experience
Some users have reported a positive experience with the Fire TV, praising its secure streaming capabilities and affordable price. However, others have encountered issues with refurbished models, which have arrived defective or failed to boot up properly. One user reported receiving two defective Fire TVs, with one displaying a corrupted screen and the other stuck in an infinite loop. Another user noted that the refurbished process and quality control at Amazon need improvement.

Despite these concerns, many users have expressed satisfaction with the Fire TV, particularly its ability to handle DD+ to DD conversion on Netflix and HBO Go, and its compatibility with a range of third-party apps. One user even noted that they were able to install XBMC (Kodi) on the device, making it a viable alternative to their HTPC.

Another user, who had previously used a Roku XS and Roku 4, praised the Fire TV for its faster navigation and ability to control smart devices directly from the remote. They also noted that the device was able to handle DD and DTS 5.1 audio, making it a significant improvement over their previous devices.

Overall, the Fire TV offers a secure and affordable streaming solution, but potential buyers should be aware of the risks associated with refurbished models. While some users have reported defects and lack of functionality, others have had positive experiences with the device and its wide range of available content.

The Kindle is my first e-ink reader. I own an iPad, an iPhone, and have owned a Windows-based phone in the past that I used as an ereader.My overall impression of the device is good.The good:I'd honestly rather read linear (read from page one to the end, one page at a time) fiction from it than a book, because I can't always get comfortable with a book. Hardcovers are sometimes a bit heavy, and paperbacks don't always lie open easily. The Kindle is incredibly light and thin. I can hold it in one hand easily. The page turn buttons are conveniently located. Page-turns aren't instant, but they're probably quicker than turning a physical page in a printed book (there are just a lot more page-turns unless you choose a small font). The contrast is better than other ereaders I've seen. There is zero eye strain in good light. My eyesight isn't the greatest and I like being able to increase the font size and read without glasses. I love being able to browse the Kindle store and read samples before deciding to purchase. The experimental browser is surprisingly usable, but isn't great. It is useful for browsing wikipedia and blogs. The biggest drawback to the browser is the awkward pointer navigation, using the 5-way pad. It syncs your furthest read page over the internet so you can pick up where you left off using your iPhone or iPad.The so-so:The kindle store could use more categories and sorting options. You can't sort by top rated, and there is no category for alternate histories, for example. Finding a very-specific type of fiction relies on keyword searches, which don't do a great job. The wifi sometimes doesn't connect before it times-out.Read more I'm a first-time Kindle owner, so I have nothing to compare the latest Kindle to. I don't own a Nook. I don't own an iPad (and, in any case, that's comparing apples to oranges). I don't have a Sony e-reader. 'This will be a short, simple review.I received my Kindle about a week ago and haven't been able to put it down.Things I like about my Kindle1. The e-ink display is amazing.2. Using the 5-way controller is simple and effective.3. Page turn speeds are faster than I thought they would be.4. It's lightweight, even with the attached cover (I have an Amazon cover with a built-in light)5. Page-turning buttons are quiet and well-placed.6. Recharge time is fast.7. I can order a book and start reading it in less than 60 seconds. Nice!8. Portability... I can take 3,000 books with me when I travel for work and not require additional suitcases or baggage fees.Things I'm not too keen on1. Buttons are too close together and are laid out oddly.2. Lack of individual number buttons is frustrating.3. Power button on the bottom Not a bad thing. Just an odd thing. (Same for the headphone input). I usually rest the bottom of a book on my lap when I read.Things I hope change in the future1. How books are organized... When I put a book in a collection (which is actually a tag), it still appears in the main list. It's not actually moved, it's merely associated.2. The look of the main screen. I'd like folders or some other way to display collections.3. Ability to create personal screen savers.4. E-book pricing, though Amazon has little control over this.Read more UPDATE NOVEMBER 2011:My review is now over a year old, as is the Kindle Keyboard as Amazon calls it now. There are newer models: the basic, cheapie Kindle and the Kindle Touch, and of course the Kindle Fire quasi-tablet.Each of these models is an excellent choice. Whichever one is right for you just depends on your preferences.The 3 e-ink Kindles are Kindle Keyboard (this one), Kindle Touch (the newest flagship model), and the basic Kindle. All 3 of them have EXACTLY THE SAME 6 DISPLAY, with the same sharp typeface and high contrast that reads like ink on paper with no eyestrain. The Kindle Keyboard is the oldest of these models, and I got one of the first ones when they came out in August 2010.I still absolutely LOVE my Kindle Keyboard and use it

response:
Headline
A captivating e-reader experience: The Kindle Keyboard offers a sharp display, easy navigation, and extensive library access.

Product summary
The Kindle Keyboard is a popular e-reader model that boasts a high-quality e-ink display, intuitive navigation, and a vast library of available books. Users praise its sharp text and high contrast, which reduces eye strain and offers a reading experience akin to traditional books.

Key features
- E-ink display: Provides a sharp and readable text, reducing eye strain.
- Navigation: Offers simple and effective 5-way controller for easy page turning.
- Library access: Allows users to access a vast collection of books through the Kindle Store.

Pros
- High-quality e-ink display
- Intuitive navigation with 5-way controller
- Extensive library access through the Kindle Store

Cons
- Buttons could be more spread out and better labeled
- Lack of individual number buttons
- Power button and headphone input located at the bottom, which may interfere with traditional reading position.

User experience
The Kindle Keyboard offers a captivating e-reader experience, with a sharp display that reads like ink on paper and reduces eye strain. Its intuitive navigation makes it easy to turn pages and access features, while its extensive library access allows users to explore a vast collection of books.

While some users have noted a few drawbacks, such as the close proximity of buttons and the lack of individual number buttons, the overall consensus is that the Kindle Keyboard is a top-tier e-reader that delivers on its promise of a high-quality reading experience.

If you're in the market for an e-reader and are looking for a device that offers a sharp display, easy navigation, and extensive library access, the Kindle Keyboard is definitely worth considering. Its high-quality e-ink display, intuitive navigation, and vast library of available books make it a popular choice among avid readers.

So, if you're looking for a device that can replace the physical books on your shelves, the Kindle Keyboard is an excellent investment. With its sharp display, easy navigation, and extensive library access, it offers a reading experience that is both convenient and enjoyable.

In summary, the Kindle Keyboard is a top-tier e-reader that delivers on its promise of a high-quality reading experience. Its sharp display, intuitive navigation, and extensive library access make it a popular choice among avid readers. While some users have noted a few drawbacks, the overall consensus is that the Kindle Keyboard is a worthwhile investment for anyone looking to make the transition from physical books to digital.

I wanted a new cord for my kindal and I wanted one like the one that came with my kindal. This was not what was as described. It's not white or anything like the original. It did ship quickly. I seldom write reviews I like my privacy. Exactly what I needed. Works well. good length cord, good value and got here quickly. Wow! This charger is amazing! It charges like lightning! None of my old chargers can hold a candle to this one. Usually charges within 10 and 30 minutes. Nice length on cord. Quality is outstanding! Would I recommend this product Absolutely! Kindle Power Adapter Wall Charger USB cable arrived sooner than expected and in excellent condition. Exactly what I was looking for. This was exactly what I needed. Knew I would find it on Ebay. I Could not be more thrilled to find a new cord for my very old Kindle reader. ,Speed of shipping awesome and quality great When I got my Kindle Fire, I got a protective cover for it and figured I'd be ok, because I don't travel much at all (am bedridden/disabled from car accidents). After a while, I couldn't get the mini-USB cord to stay in the plug on the Kindle. After describing the problem to Amazon, they sent me a new cord (I got the newer one with an AC plug and had to pay for it). Using my new cable (purchased through the Amazon Kindle store), I had no problems, at first.With only very light use, primarily for reading books for short intervals, the problem I had before with the mini USB plug on the Kindle not fitting tightly into its socket. After changing up AC to USB, as well as trying both USBs on my computer, I was unable to get the Kindle to start because it had less than 15 charge on the battery but I had no way to charge it. The only way to resolve the issue would be to go through the procedure described above I read a number of the positive reviews (which are usually posted first), and nothing was mentioned about this problem--there were so many reviews I didn't have the time to go through them all. I'm in the middle of a book, and use the Kindle to play Scrabble with a friend, and we're in the middle of a game...So I haven't decided what to do yet... Headline
A user's experience with the Fire TV: Easy setup, excellent image quality, and convenient features.

Product summary
The Fire TV offers a no-brainer setup process, excellent image quality for movies and games, and a straightforward navigation system. Users praise the device's simplicity and ease of use, though some express disappointment with the lack of a way to filter content available for Amazon Prime members.

Key features
- Easy setup: Setup was straightforward and required minimal time.
- Excellent image quality: Movies and games display in high quality.
- User-friendly navigation: Navigation is simple and efficient.
- Voice recognition remote: The remote's voice recognition feature works well.

Pros
- Straightforward setup process
- High-quality image output
- Convenient voice recognition remote

Cons
- Limited options for filtering Amazon Prime content

User experience
The Fire TV proved to be an enjoyable addition to my home entertainment system. Setting it up was a breeze, taking only about 15 minutes from unboxing to having it in action. The image quality was impressive, with movies and games displaying in near Blu-Ray quality. Navigation was also a plus, as I found it easy to locate content and install apps and games. The remote was another standout feature, with well-designed buttons and a responsive voice recognition function. My only complaint was the lack of a way to filter content available for Amazon Prime members. I had hoped to be able to easily differentiate between free and paid content, but instead found myself searching through a mix of both. This was somewhat inconvenient, as I often wanted to watch free content but had to sift through paid options to find it. Overall, I was pleased with my Fire TV experience and would recommend it to others. However, I do hope that Amazon will address the issue of filtering Prime content in future updates.

Overall, I would recommend this Kindle Lighted Leather Cover to anyone in the market for a high-quality and functional cover for their Kindle. While it may not be perfect, its attractive design, lightweight construction, and effective built-in light make it a worthwhile investment for avid readers.

Headline
A portable and fun Amazon Echo device with excellent sound quality but limited connectivity options.

Product summary
The Amazon Echo Tap is a compact and entertaining device that offers excellent sound quality and voice control capabilities. However, some users have reported difficulties in connecting to public Wi-Fi networks and using it as a standalone device.

Key features
- Portable design: Allows users to take the device from room to room and use it as a Bluetooth speaker.
- Voice control: Enables users to ask questions, play music, and control smart home devices using voice commands.
- Excellent sound quality: Provides clear and powerful audio output.

Pros
- Compact and portable design
- Excellent sound quality
- Fun and entertaining voice control features

Cons
- Difficulty in connecting to public Wi-Fi networks
- Limited functionality without a phone or home Wi-Fi connection
- Slightly more expensive than some standalone Bluetooth speakers

User experience
The Amazon Echo Tap is a compact and portable version of the popular Amazon Echo smart speaker. It offers users the ability to take the device from room to room and use it as a Bluetooth speaker, as well as the voice control capabilities that have made the Echo so popular. One of the standout features of the Tap is its excellent sound quality, which is on par with much larger and more expensive speakers. Users have reported that the Tap delivers clear and powerful audio output, making it an ideal choice for those who enjoy listening to music or podcasts.

However, some users have reported issues with the Tap's connectivity options. While it is easy to connect the Tap to a home Wi-Fi network or a phone's Bluetooth connection, some users have had difficulty connecting to public Wi-Fi networks. Additionally, the Tap requires a phone or home Wi-Fi connection to access many of its features, such as streaming music or checking the weather. This can be a limitation for users who do not have constant access to these networks.

Despite these limitations, the Amazon Echo Tap is still a fun and entertaining device. Its voice control capabilities are a major selling point, allowing users to ask questions, play music, and control smart home devices using only their voice. The Tap also has a sleek and attractive design, with a fabric cover that feels nice in the hand and does not slip easily. Overall, the Tap is a great choice for those who want the convenience of a portable smart speaker with excellent sound quality and voice control capabilities. - Easy to use and navigate
- Long battery life, saving space and the need for frequent charging
- Comfortable reading experience with adjustable light feature

Cons
- Occasional technical issues with footnotes and highlighting
- Some users find the touch screen buttons too sensitive, leading to accidental page turns or home screen returns

Overall, the Kindle Paperwhite is a high-quality e-reader that offers a comfortable and convenient reading experience. Its long battery life and adjustable light feature make it a great choice for avid readers, while its user-friendly interface and touch screen controls make it easy to use. However, some users have reported occasional technical issues with footnotes and highlighting, and others find the touch screen buttons too sensitive, leading to accidental page turns or home screen returns. Despite these minor drawbacks, the Kindle Paperwhite is a worthwhile investment for anyone looking for a reliable and convenient e-reader.
